candidates.why: Separate operating D&A summary exists above EBIT, but it is not labelled as COGS/cost of sales and appears to be a broad operating expense line rather than a COGS-specific add-on. Using it for COGS would risk misallocation and double counting.

Separate operating depreciation line exists, but it is not identified as relating to cost of goods sold and appears to be part of a broader nature-of-expense disclosure rather than a COGS-specific row.
Could include production assets, but the statement gives no explicit link to the selected COGS bucket. Assigning it to COGS would be speculative.
Separate D&A component is disclosed, but there is no indication it belongs specifically to cost of goods sold.
Separate operating amortization component is disclosed, but it is not presented as part of COGS and seems more likely tied to broader operating or R&D/admin activities.
Patent amortization is disclosed separately, but there is no evidence it should be added specifically to COGS.
This appears R&D-related rather than COGS-related, so it should not be assigned to the selected COGS bucket.
Label is too generic and not tied to cost of goods sold.

variables.notes: No D&A row is explicitly linked to cost of goods sold. The selected COGS row is a functional expense line, while the disclosed D&A rows appear as separate by-nature operating expenses. Conservatively leave COGS_DA empty to avoid misallocating broad operating D&A to COGS.

candidates.why: Separate operating D&A summary exists above EBIT, but it is a company-wide summary and includes impairment. It is not clearly an SG&A-only add-on relative to the selected Selling, Administrative, and R&D buckets.
Separate operating depreciation line exists, but it is not labelled as SG&A/admin/selling and cannot be cleanly assigned to the selected XSGA bucket.
Separate component is disclosed, but no wording ties it specifically to SG&A/admin/selling.
Could relate partly to office/admin functions, but the statement gives no explicit SG&A attribution.
Separate amortization component exists, but it is not shown as SG&A-specific and may overlap with R&D-related intangibles.
Patent amortization is disclosed, but not explicitly as SG&A. It may be part of R&D-related amortization instead.

This is the clearest R&D-related D&A row, but the statement already shows a separate above-EBIT 'Research and development expenses' row, and that same row was selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD. Per the R&D interaction rule, do not add this separately.
Generic amortization component with no explicit SG&A linkage.

variables.notes: Manually Changed. Although separate D&A rows are disclosed above EBIT, none is clearly attributable to SG&A-only. The one specifically suggestive of R&D ('Amortisation of Completed Development Projects') should not be added because 'Research and development expenses' is already separately selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD, so adding R&D amortization separately could distort the intended XSGA minus XRD logic. Conservatively leave XSGA_DA empty.
